Numerical Ability.shortcut: If an item is sold at a profit of x% and then a loss of x%, there is always a net loss equivalent to (x^2 / 100)%.
To double your money at r% interest, rule of 72: T = 72 / r.
The difference between CI and SI for exactly 2 years is: Difference = P * (R/100)^2

Numerical Ability.formula: If A can do work in x days, A's 1 day work = 1/x
Numerical Ability.shortcut: If A takes 'x' days and B takes 'y' days, together they take (x*y) / (x+y) days.
To convert km/hr to m/s, multiply by 5/18. To convert m/s to km/hr, multiply by 18/5.

Numerical Ability.formula: Train passing a pole: Dist = Length of Train. Train passing a platform: Dist = Length of Train + Length of Platform.
Numerical Ability.shortcut: Relative Speed: If moving in opposite directions, speeds Add (v1+v2). If same direction, speeds Subtract |v1-v2|.

Reasoning Ability.shortcut: All A are B -> Some B are A. No A is B -> No B is A. If premises are Positive, Negative conclusions are mostly false.
Use squares for Males, circles for Females. Horizontal lines for siblings, vertical for generations. Father's sister = Aunt, Mother's brother = Uncle.
If a person faces North and turns right, they face East. If they make multiple turns, add right turns (+90) and left turns (-90) together.

Reasoning Ability.formula: Hour hand moves 0.5 degrees/min. Minute hand moves 6 degrees/min.
Reasoning Ability.shortcut: Angle between hands = |30 * H - 5.5 * M| degrees.

Programming Concepts.formula: O(1) < O(log N) < O(N) < O(N log N) < O(N^2) < O(2^N)
Programming Concepts.shortcut: Loops determine time complexity. Single loop = O(N); Nested loops = O(N^2); Dividing range in half (Binary Search) = O(log N).

Programming Concepts.formula: AND (&), OR (|), XOR (^), NOT (~), Left Shift (<<), Right Shift (>>)
Programming Concepts.shortcut: XORing a number with itself is 0 (x^x = 0). Left shift (<< 1) multiplies by 2. Right shift (>> 1) divides by 2.
If calculating factorials > 12 or Fibonacci > 46, always use 64-bit integer values (long long).

Coding / DSA.shortcut: For sum queries on subarrays, pre-calculate a Prefix Sum array to resolve queries in O(1) time instead of O(N).

Coding / DSA.formula: Linear Search: O(N), Binary Search: O(log N)
Coding / DSA.shortcut: Binary search ONLY works on sorted arrays. Mid calculation safely: mid = left + (right - left) / 2 to avoid overflow.

Coding / DSA.formula: Bubble/Insertion/Selection: O(n^2), Merge/Quick: O(n log n)
Coding / DSA.shortcut: When elements are strictly small integers, use Counting Sort for linear O(N) time.
In-Order Traversal of a Binary Search Tree (BST) visits nodes in strictly ascending sorted order.

Verbal Ability.shortcut: Often the main idea of a passage is explicitly stated in the first (introductory) or last (concluding) paragraph.
Singular subjects take verbs with an 's' (The dog runs). Plural subjects take verbs without an 's' (The dogs run). Also check tenses consistently.
If you don't know the word, isolate the prefix or suffix (e.g. 'anti-' means against, '-less' means without) or deduce tone (positive/negative) from the sentence.
